name: Sunnyvale Recycling & Compost Center
description: Free compost and mulch for Sunnyvale residents. Drop off garden waste and pick up finished compost — no ID check required for pickup. Great for enriching Bay Area clay soils.

name: Valley Water Landscape Rebate Program
description: Up to $3,000 residential / $100,000 commercial rebates for converting lawn to drought-tolerant landscaping, installing drip irrigation, or capturing rainwater. Santa Clara County residents only.

name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
description: Non-profit park with heritage rose garden, historic orchard, and community garden. Hosts plant swaps, succulent workshops, and volunteer days. Great place to connect with Bay Area gardeners.

name: Indian Plants N Seeds
description: One-stop online shop for South Asian vegetable and herb seeds — tulsi, ajwain, karela, kothimeera, and dozens of Indian variety brinjal, chilli, and leafy greens. Ships to all US states.

name: Seeds of India
description: 25-year-old company shipping non-GMO South Asian seeds and live plants including curry leaf, jasmine, and seasonal vegetables. Same/next-day shipping to continental US.

name: Neem Tree Farms
description: America's oldest neem company — ships living neem trees and Ayurvedic plants alongside cold-pressed neem oil for natural pest control. Excellent source for hard-to-find Indian medicinal plants.

name: Ten Mile Creek Nursery
description: The most comprehensive US source for lotus rhizomes — over 100 varieties shipped bare-root nationwide. Certified sustainable through Union for Ethical Biotrade.

name: Petaluma Seed Bank (Baker Creek)
description: Northern California outpost of Baker Creek Heirloom Seeds — the largest heirloom seed collection in the US. Drop in for rare heirloom varieties or order online. Great for preserving South Asian food traditions with authentic seed varieties.

name: Logee's Plants
description: Specialty online nursery for tropical and sub-tropical plants — an excellent source for citrus, plumeria, hibiscus, and South Asian fruit trees that are hard to find locally.

name: Vivasayi Garden
description: Online source for South Indian medicinal and culinary plants including karpooravalli (omavalli), tulsi varieties, and hard-to-find Indian herbs. Ships live plants.

name: Grow Organic Club
description: Bay Area-rooted online community for organic and home gardeners. Plant swaps, seed sharing, growing tips, and connections with local growers. Great for sourcing cuttings from other Bay Area gardeners.

name: Houzz Zone 9 Plumeria Forum
description: Active community discussion with Bay Area gardeners sharing real tips on growing plumeria in Northern CA Zone 9 — which varieties survive, overwintering strategies, and where to source plants locally.
